title: Son of a Witch
anchor_summary: Co-op action roguelike dungeon crawler built around procedural runs, class-based heroes, item/weapon synergies, boss rooms, and up-to-4-player drop-in play.

similar_games.title: Lost Castle
similar_games.why_similar: 4-player co-op action roguelike with procedural dungeon runs, class/build variation, lots of loot, and beat-'em-up style combat.

similar_games.title: Streets of Rogue
similar_games.why_similar: Run-based co-op roguelite with unlockable classes, heavy item interactions, chaotic room-to-room encounters, and strong emergent build variety.

similar_games.title: Heroes of Hammerwatch
similar_games.why_similar: Co-op fantasy dungeon-crawling roguelite focused on procedural runs, character classes, loot, and clearing combat rooms with bosses.

similar_games.title: Gauntlet: Slayer Edition
similar_games.why_similar: Arcade-style 4-player fantasy dungeon crawler centered on class roles, room combat, loot, and cooperative mob clearing.

similar_games.title: Ember Knights
similar_games.why_similar: Fast co-op action roguelite with procedural runs, class-like weapon/spell builds, boss fights, and multiplayer-focused dungeon progression.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify Son of a Witch as an 'action roguelike for 1-4 players' built around procedurally generated dungeon runs, permadeath, co-op play, random encounters, many bosses, and heavy item/weapon/spell synergy. The core loop is run-based action combat with replayable procedural content and cooperative play, which strongly fits co_op_action_roguelite. Combat is dominant and hybrid, with explicit melee, magic shooter, and ranged archery styles. Bosses, random rooms, unlockable characters, pets, and item synergies reinforce a roguelite action identity rather than a pure RPG or party game classification.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: 4-player co-op action roguelike with procedural dungeon runs, class/build variation, lots of loot, and beat-'em-up style combat.
Run-based co-op roguelite with unlockable classes, heavy item interactions, chaotic room-to-room encounters, and strong emergent build variety.
Co-op fantasy dungeon-crawling roguelite focused on procedural runs, character classes, loot, and clearing combat rooms with bosses.
Arcade-style 4-player fantasy dungeon crawler centered on class roles, room combat, loot, and cooperative mob clearing.
Fast co-op action roguelite with procedural runs, class-like weapon/spell builds, boss fights, and multiplayer-focused dungeon progression.

title: Mordheim: City of the Damned - Witch Hunters
anchor_summary: Standalone/additive DLC for Mordheim: City of the Damned that keeps the parent game's core identity: turn-based tactical warband battles, persistent unit building, campaign progression, and harsh injury/permadeath-style roster management.

similar_games.title: Mordheim: City of the Damned
similar_games.why_similar: The DLC directly inherits the base game's turn-based skirmish combat, warband progression, equipment loadouts, and campaign structure.

similar_games.title: Necromunda: Underhive Wars
similar_games.why_similar: Another Games Workshop skirmish tactics game built around small persistent gangs, unit customization, and campaign-style progression between brutal battles.

similar_games.title: Battle Brothers
similar_games.why_similar: Shares squad-scale turn-based combat, roster development, injuries/losses, gear management, and long-term campaign decisions.

similar_games.title: XCOM 2
similar_games.why_similar: A recognizable benchmark for turn-based squad tactics with persistent soldiers, ability progression, and high-stakes mission planning.

similar_games.title: Warhammer 40,000: Chaos Gate - Daemonhunters
similar_games.why_similar: Closer on Warhammer-flavored tactical combat and squad progression, with turn-based missions centered on specialized units and ability builds.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence identifies this as DLC ('This Warband adds the Witch Hunters') and provides faction/lore/unit-loadout details, but it does not establish the parent game's core gameplay structure strongly enough on its own. The text suggests strategy/RPG elements, tactical acumen, campaign content, weapons, skills, and battles, yet lacks reliable evidence for required taxonomy-defining axes such as perspective, battle format, turn structure, world topology, and interface/control model. Because likely_parent_game is not supplied, parent evidence cannot be inherited for this DLC, so the gameplay identity remains under-supported.
